symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly enlarging pigmented mole over 3 months
symptoms.character: progressive enlargement of a pre-existing naevus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Evolution/change (the 'E' of ABCDE) is one of the strongest history features of melanoma.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: benign melanocytic naevus with mechanical trauma
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stable benign moles do not enlarge rapidly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Asymmetric lesion with irregular notched borders and variegated black/brown/grey colour, diameter ~1.2 cm
symptoms.character: ABCDE surface morphology visible to the patient on a phone photo

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Asymmetry, border irregularity, colour variegation and diameter >6 mm are the core ABCDE criteria; patient-visible on a photo but not a substitute for dermoscopy.
A uniform waxy 'stuck-on' plaque lacks variegated colour and notched borders.

symptoms.symptom_name: New central ulceration with persistent venous oozing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ulceration of a pigmented lesion signals an advanced/invasive tumour with friable neovascularity and is an adverse prognostic marker.

symptoms.symptom_name: New localised pruritus of the lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New itch or altered sensation in a changing pigmented lesion is a recognised soft symptom of malignant change.

symptoms.symptom_name: 'Ugly duckling' sign — lesion distinct from all other moles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A lesion that stands out from a patient's other naevi is a validated high-yield melanoma screening cue.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm, mobile, non-tender left axillary lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Firm regional nodal enlargement in the draining basin suggests lymphatic metastatic spread — upstages disease. The patient can self-report a lump, but clinical characterisation (firmness, mobility, tenderness) requires a clinician and is sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic solar damage of surrounding skin / intense youth sun exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cumulative and intense intermittent UV exposure is the principal environmental risk factor for cutaneous melanoma.
Excludes drug-induced coagulopathy as the cause of bleeding — the bleeding is from the friable tumour surface, not a bleeding diathesis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No lightheadedness, faintness, breathlessness or palpitations

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of symptoms of hypovolaemia supports haemodynamic stability — the bleed is a controllable venous ooze, not exsanguinating haemorrhage; keeps the honest tier at T3 rather than T4/T5.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP 125/80, HR 88, Temp 36.5 C, RR 16, SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective haemodynamic stability — a sealed clinician measurement. The AI Doctor infers stability from the patient's absence of faintness/dizziness, not from a measured BP, and must not present these numbers as its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excisional biopsy showing atypical melanocytic proliferation (anticipated confirmatory histology)

investigation_details.expected_result: Atypical melanocytic proliferation consistent with invasive melanoma; Breslow thickness, ulceration and mitotic rate for staging
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without histology the diagnosis cannot be confirmed or staged and definitive excision is delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histopathology is the definitive/confirmatory test — never available via telehealth in real time; it is the sealed answer key, not an AI output.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: ABCDE melanoma constellation with ulceration and regional node
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these features constitute a high-suspicion pattern mandating urgent specialist excision and staging; no single feature is required, but the cluster is compelling.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent specialist dermoscopy and excisional biopsy with histopathology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive diagnosis, Breslow thickness, ulceration and staging — the essential next step; must be arranged by referral, not performed or interpreted by the AI Doctor.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Specialist evaluation of the axillary node (ultrasound +/- fine-needle biopsy / sentinel-node assessment)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess suspected nodal metastatic spread as part of staging.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Partial or shave biopsy of the pigmented lesion in primary care
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Partial/shave sampling of a suspected melanoma is discouraged — it compromises Breslow staging; excisional biopsy by the specialist pathway is preferred.
